axes[0].set_yticks(range(len(NORM_STRATEGIES)))
axes[0].set_yticklabels(
    [
        ns.replace("softmax_", "T=").replace("rank_normalized", "rank")
        for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
    ],
    fontsize=9,
)
axes[0].set_title("Precision@40% Retain")
plt.colorbar(im1, ax=axes[0])
for i in range(len(NORM_STRATEGIES)):
    for j in range(len(ds_names)):
        axes[0].text(
            j, i, f"{prec_matrix[i,j]:.3f}", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8
        )

im2 = axes[1].imshow(rec_matrix, cmap="YlOrRd", aspect="auto", vmin=0, vmax=1)
axes[1].set_xticks(range(len(ds_names)))
axes[1].set_xticklabels(
    [ds.replace("synthetic_", "") for ds in ds_names], rotation=20, fontsize=9
)
axes[1].set_yticks(range(len(NORM_STRATEGIES)))
axes[1].set_yticklabels(
    [
        ns.replace("softmax_", "T=").replace("rank_normalized", "rank")
        for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
    ],
    fontsize=9,
)
axes[1].set_title("Recall@40% Retain")
plt.colorbar(im2, ax=axes[1])
for i in range(len(NORM_STRATEGIES)):
    for j in range(len(ds_names)):
        axes[1].text(
            j, i, f"{rec_matrix[i,j]:.3f}", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8
        )
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "precision_recall_heatmap.png"), dpi=100)
plt.close()
print("Saved precision_recall_heatmap.png")

# Plot 5: Summary - relative accuracy at 40% retain
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ds_names), figsize=(6 * len(ds_names), 5))
for col, ds_name in enumerate(ds_names):
    ax = axes[col]
    d = all_results[ds_name]
    full_acc = d["full_acc"]
    rel_accs = [
        d["norm_results"][ns]["eviction_accs"][0.40] / full_acc
        for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
    ]
    att_rel = d["baseline_results"]["attention"]["eviction_accs"][0.40] / full_acc
    rand_rel = d["baseline_results"]["random"]["eviction_accs"][0.40] / full_acc
    bar_labels = [
        ns.replace("softmax_", "T=").replace("rank_normalized", "rank")
        for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
    ]
    bar_colors = [colors_norm[ns] for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES]
    bars = ax.bar(bar_labels, rel_accs, color=bar_colors, alpha=0.85)
    ax.axhline(1.0, color="black", linestyle="--", label="Full Cache", linewidth=1.5)
    ax.axhline(
        att_rel,
        color="gray",
        linestyle="-.",
        label=f"Attention ({att_rel:.3f})",
        linewidth=1.5,
    )
    ax.axhline(
        rand_rel,
        color="lightgray",
        linestyle=":",
        label=f"Random ({rand_rel:.3f})",
        linewidth=1.5,
    )
    for bar, val in zip(bars, rel_accs):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.005,
            f"{val:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=9,
        )
    ax.set_title(f"Relative Acc @40% Retain\n{ds_name}", fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylabel("Relative Accuracy")
    ax.set_ylim([0, 1.15])
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=15)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "summary_norm_ablation.png"), dpi=100)
plt.close()
print("Saved summary_norm_ablation.png")

# Save all experiment data
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)
print("\nAll experiment data saved.")

print("\n=== FINAL RESULTS: Routing Score Normalization Strategy Ablation ===")
for ds_name in ds_names:
    r = all_results[ds_name]
    print(f"\n[{ds_name}]")
    print(
        f"  Full-cache acc: {r['full_acc']:.4f}, Best val acc: {r['best_val_acc']:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"  {'Norm Strategy':<20} {'Acc@25%':>8} {'Acc@40%':>8} {'Acc@60%':>8} {'Prec@40%':>10} {'EntGap':>8}"
    )
    print(f"  {'-'*70}")
    for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES:
        nr = r["norm_results"][ns]
        accs = [f"{nr['eviction_accs'][rr]:.4f}" for rr in [0.25, 0.40, 0.60]]
        prec = nr["precision_40"]
        gap = nr["entropy"]["gap"]
        print(
            f"  {ns:<20} {accs[0]:>8} {accs[1]:>8} {accs[2]:>8} {prec:>10.4f} {gap:>8.4f}"
        )
    for bl in BASELINES:
        br = r["baseline_results"][bl]
        accs = [f"{br['eviction_accs'][rr]:.4f}" for rr in [0.25, 0.40, 0.60]]
        print(f"  {bl:<20} {accs[0]:>8} {accs[1]:>8} {accs[2]:>8}")
plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

NORM_STRATEGIES = ["softmax_t0.5", "softmax_t1.0", "softmax_t2.0", "rank_normalized"]
BASELINES = ["attention", "random"]
RETAIN_RATIOS = [0.25, 0.40, 0.60, 0.75]
ds_names = list(experiment_data.keys())
colors_norm = {
    "softmax_t0.5": "steelblue",
    "softmax_t1.0": "darkorange",
    "softmax_t2.0": "seagreen",
    "rank_normalized": "purple",
}

# Plot 1: Training/Validation Loss and Accuracy Curves per dataset
for ds_name in ds_names:
    try:
        d = experiment_data[ds_name]
        epochs_range = list(range(1, len(d["train_accs"]) + 1))
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
        fig.suptitle(f"Training Curves — Dataset: {ds_name}", fontsize=13)
        axes[0].plot(
            epochs_range, d["train_losses"], label="Train Loss", color="steelblue"
        )
        axes[0].plot(
            epochs_range, d["val_losses"], label="Val Loss", color="darkorange"
        )
        axes[0].set_title("Loss over Epochs")
        axes[0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
        axes[0].set_ylabel("Loss")
        axes[0].legend()
        axes[1].plot(
            epochs_range, d["train_accs"], label="Train Acc", color="steelblue"
        )
        axes[1].plot(epochs_range, d["val_accs"], label="Val Acc", color="darkorange")
        axes[1].set_title("Accuracy over Epochs")
        axes[1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
        axes[1].set_ylabel("Accuracy")
        axes[1].legend()
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"training_curves_{ds_name}.png"), dpi=100
        )
        plt.close()
        print(f"Saved training_curves_{ds_name}.png")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating training curves for {ds_name}: {e}")
        plt.close()

# Plot 2: Compression accuracy curves by norm strategy per dataset
for ds_name in ds_names:
    try:
        d = experiment_data[ds_name]
        full_acc = d["full_acc"]
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 6))
        ax.axhline(
            full_acc,
            color="black",
            linestyle="--",
            linewidth=2,
            label=f"Full Cache ({full_acc:.4f})",
        )
        for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES:
            accs = [d["norm_results"][ns]["eviction_accs"][rr] for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS]
            ax.plot(
                RETAIN_RATIOS,
                accs,
                marker="o",
                label=f"RouteKV ({ns})",
                color=colors_norm[ns],
                linewidth=2,
            )
        for bl, ls in [("attention", "--"), ("random", ":")]:
            accs = [
                d["baseline_results"][bl]["eviction_accs"][rr] for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS
            ]
            ax.plot(
                RETAIN_RATIOS,
                accs,
                marker="s",
                label=bl,
                linestyle=ls,
                color="gray",
                linewidth=1.5,
            )
        ax.set_xlabel("Retain Ratio (KV Budget)", fontsize=12)
        ax.set_ylabel("Accuracy", fontsize=12)
        ax.set_title(
            f"Norm Strategy Compression Comparison\nDataset: {ds_name}", fontsize=13
        )
        ax.legend(fontsize=9)
        ax.set_xlim([0.2, 0.8])
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"compression_norm_{ds_name}.png"), dpi=100
        )
        plt.close()
        print(f"Saved compression_norm_{ds_name}.png")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating compression plot for {ds_name}: {e}")
        plt.close()

# Plot 3: Entropy gap comparison across datasets
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ds_names), figsize=(6 * len(ds_names), 5))
    fig.suptitle("Routing Entropy by Norm Strategy (Key vs Filler Tokens)", fontsize=13)
    for col, ds_name in enumerate(ds_names):
        ax = axes[col]
        d = experiment_data[ds_name]
        key_ents = [d["norm_results"][ns]["entropy"]["key"] for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES]
        filler_ents = [
            d["norm_results"][ns]["entropy"]["filler"] for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
        ]
        gaps = [d["norm_results"][ns]["entropy"]["gap"] for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES]
        x = np.arange(len(NORM_STRATEGIES))
        width = 0.28
        ax.bar(
            x - width,
            key_ents,
            width,
            label="Key Entropy",
            color="steelblue",
            alpha=0.8,
        )
        ax.bar(
            x, filler_ents, width, label="Filler Entropy", color="darkorange", alpha=0.8
        )
        ax.bar(
            x + width,
            gaps,
            width,
            label="Gap (filler-key)",
            color="seagreen",
            alpha=0.8,
        )
        ax.set_xticks(x)
        ax.set_xticklabels(
            [
                ns.replace("softmax_", "T=").replace("rank_normalized", "rank")
                for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
            ],
            rotation=15,
            fontsize=8,
        )
        ax.set_title(f'Dataset: {ds_name.replace("synthetic_", "")}', fontsize=11)
        ax.set_ylabel("Entropy")
        ax.axhline(0, color="red", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "entropy_gap_by_norm_all_datasets.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved entropy_gap_by_norm_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating entropy gap plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 4: Precision/Recall heatmaps at 40% retain
try:
    prec_matrix = np.zeros((len(NORM_STRATEGIES), len(ds_names)))
    rec_matrix = np.zeros((len(NORM_STRATEGIES), len(ds_names)))
    for j, ds_name in enumerate(ds_names):
        for i, ns in enumerate(NORM_STRATEGIES):
            prec_matrix[i, j] = experiment_data[ds_name]["norm_results"][ns][
                "precision_40"
            ]
            rec_matrix[i, j] = experiment_data[ds_name]["norm_results"][ns]["recall_40"]
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
    fig.suptitle(
        "Key Token Eviction Precision & Recall @ 40% Retain Ratio", fontsize=13
    )
    xtick_labels = [ds.replace("synthetic_", "") for ds in ds_names]
    ytick_labels = [
        ns.replace("softmax_", "T=").replace("rank_normalized", "rank")
        for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
    ]
    for ax, matrix, title in zip(
        axes, [prec_matrix, rec_matrix], ["Precision@40%", "Recall@40%"]
    ):
        im = ax.imshow(matrix, cmap="YlOrRd", aspect="auto", vmin=0, vmax=1)
        ax.set_xticks(range(len(ds_names)))
        ax.set_xticklabels(xtick_labels, rotation=20, fontsize=9)
        ax.set_yticks(range(len(NORM_STRATEGIES)))
        ax.set_yticklabels(ytick_labels, fontsize=9)
        ax.set_title(title)
        plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax)
        for i in range(len(NORM_STRATEGIES)):
            for j in range(len(ds_names)):
                ax.text(
                    j, i, f"{matrix[i,j]:.3f}", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=9
                )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "precision_recall_heatmap_all_datasets.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved precision_recall_heatmap_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating precision/recall heatmap: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 5: Relative accuracy at 40% retain — summary bar chart
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ds_names), figsize=(6 * len(ds_names), 5))
    fig.suptitle(
        "Relative Accuracy @ 40% Retain Ratio vs Full Cache (All Strategies)",
        fontsize=13,
    )
    for col, ds_name in enumerate(ds_names):
        ax = axes[col]
        d = experiment_data[ds_name]
        full_acc = d["full_acc"]
        rel_accs = [
            d["norm_results"][ns]["eviction_accs"][0.40] / full_acc
            for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
        ]
        bar_labels = [
            ns.replace("softmax_", "T=").replace("rank_normalized", "rank")
            for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES
        ]
        bar_colors = [colors_norm[ns] for ns in NORM_STRATEGIES]
        bars = ax.bar(bar_labels, rel_accs, color=bar_colors, alpha=0.85)
        att_rel = d["baseline_results"]["attention"]["eviction_accs"][0.40] / full_acc
        rand_rel = d["baseline_results"]["random"]["eviction_accs"][0.40] / full_acc
        ax.axhline(
            1.0, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.5, label="Full Cache (1.0)"
        )
        ax.axhline(
            att_rel,
            color="gray",
            linestyle="-.",
            linewidth=1.5,
            label=f"Attention ({att_rel:.3f})",
        )
        ax.axhline(
            rand_rel,
            color="lightgray",
            linestyle=":",
            linewidth=1.5,
            label=f"Random ({rand_rel:.3f})",
        )
        for bar, val in zip(bars, rel_accs):
            ax.text(
                bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
                bar.get_height() + 0.005,
                f"{val:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="bottom",
                fontsize=9,
            )
        ax.set_title(f'Dataset: {ds_name.replace("synthetic_", "")}', fontsize=11)
        ax.set_ylabel("Relative Accuracy")
        ax.set_ylim([0, 1.15])
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)
        ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=15)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "summary_relative_acc_40pct_all_datasets.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved summary_relative_acc_40pct_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating summary plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 6: Full accuracy vs best val accuracy per dataset
try:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
    x = np.arange(len(ds_names))
    width = 0.35
    full_accs = [experiment_data[ds]["full_acc"] for ds in ds_names]
    best_val_accs = [experiment_data[ds]["best_val_acc"] for ds in ds_names]
    ax.bar(
        x - width / 2,
        full_accs,
        width,
        label="Full-Cache Test Acc",
        color="steelblue",
        alpha=0.85,
    )
    ax.bar(
        x + width / 2,
        best_val_accs,
        width,
        label="Best Val Acc",
        color="darkorange",
        alpha=0.85,
    )
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels([ds.replace("synthetic_", "") for ds in ds_names], fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("Accuracy")
    ax.set_ylim([0, 1.1])
    ax.set_title(
        "Full-Cache Test Accuracy vs Best Validation Accuracy per Dataset", fontsize=12
    )
    ax.legend()
    for i, (fa, bva) in enumerate(zip(full_accs, best_val_accs)):
        ax.text(i - width / 2, fa + 0.01, f"{fa:.3f}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
        ax.text(i + width / 2, bva + 0.01, f"{bva:.3f}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "full_vs_val_acc_all_datasets.png"), dpi=100)
    plt.close()
    print("Saved full_vs_val_acc_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating full vs val acc plot: {e}")
    plt.close()
plot_plan: I'll analyze the experiment data structure and create visualizations for this MoE (Mixture of Experts) KV cache eviction normalization ablation study. The data contains results across 3 synthetic datasets (frontloaded, scattered, multiclass) with 4 normalization strategies (softmax at different temperatures and rank-normalized) compared against attention and random baselines. I'll plot: training/validation curves per dataset, compression accuracy curves by norm strategy, entropy gap bar charts, precision/recall heatmaps, and a summary relative accuracy comparison at 40% retain ratio. Each plot will be in a separate try-except block to handle any missing data gracefully.

plot_analyses.analysis: Across the three “Compression Curves (Top-1)” panels (synthetic/sst2/ag_news), all methods (routekv, attention, random, hybrid) sit exactly at 1.0 accuracy for all retain ratios shown (~0.25, 0.4, 0.6, 0.75). This indicates no measurable accuracy sensitivity to KV eviction in this setup, making it impossible to distinguish the contribution of routing-based scoring vs attention/random vs hybrid on these tasks. It strongly suggests the tasks are too easy, the evaluation metric is saturated, or the eviction is not actually affecting the model’s usable context (e.g., cache not used, eviction not applied, or sequence lengths too short).

The “Accuracy Degradation” panels mirror the compression curves: degradation (full − evicted) is identically 0 for all retain ratios and all strategies on synthetic/sst2/ag_news. This is consistent with either (a) true robustness at these budgets or (b) an experimental/implementation issue where evicted and full-cache runs are effectively identical. Given that even random eviction shows zero degradation at 25% retain, the latter is plausible. A quick sanity check would be to push retain ratio much lower (e.g., 5–10%), increase context length, or verify that KV tensors are actually being truncated/compacted and that attention cannot access evicted positions.

In “TopK Strategies @ 40% Budget”, relative accuracy is 1.0 for topk_routekv, topk_attention, and topk_random across synthetic/sst2/ag_news. Again, no separation between strategies. This plot is useful as a sanity indicator: if random performs identically to informed methods, the benchmark likely doesn’t stress long-range dependencies or the eviction is not impacting attention.

“Threshold RouteKV: Acc vs Adaptive Budget” shows relative accuracy at ~1.0 for all three datasets, with effective retain ratio appearing essentially unchanged/degenerate (x-axis annotation suggests tiny variation; points cluster). This implies the thresholding mechanism is not producing a meaningful adaptive budget range (or the plotted x-scale is off). If thresholding is intended to vary the number of kept tokens, you should report the distribution of effective retain ratios across examples and ensure the threshold actually gates tokens (e.g., histogram of kept fraction).

“Relative Accuracy @ 40% Retain Ratio vs Full Cache (All Strategies)” on frontloaded/scattered/multiclass: the T-based variants (T=0.5/1.0/2.0) all achieve ~1.0 relative accuracy, while the “rank” variant collapses to very low relative accuracy (~0.223, ~0.260, ~0.157). This is the first plot showing a strong ablation effect: whatever ‘rank’ does (likely ranking tokens by entropy in the opposite direction, or using a flawed ordering), it catastrophically evicts important tokens. Meanwhile, the T variants appear equivalent and robust at 40% retain. This suggests (i) the main method is insensitive to temperature in this range, and (ii) the ranking baseline is either inverted or mis-implemented.

The heatmaps “Key Token Eviction Precision & Recall @ 40% Retain Ratio” show for T=0.5/1.0/2.0: precision is constant at 0.474 across all datasets, and recall is 1.0 across all datasets; for “rank”, both precision and recall are 0. This indicates the T-based methods retain all key tokens (no false negatives), but also retain many non-key tokens (precision < 0.5), which is expected when keeping 40% of tokens and key tokens are a minority. The ‘rank’ method retaining zero key tokens explains its accuracy collapse. The constancy across datasets/temperatures is suspiciously uniform; it may reflect a synthetic key-token labeling scheme with fixed proportions or a bug that yields identical selection masks.

“Routing Entropy by Norm Strategy (Key vs Filler Tokens)” shows a consistent pattern for T=0.5/1.0/2.0: key-token entropy is much lower than filler-token entropy, with a positive gap (filler − key) around ~1.0 at low T and decreasing to ~0.6 at T=2.0. This supports the core hypothesis that routing entropy separates key vs filler tokens (key tokens are more specialized/low-entropy). However, the “rank” condition shows key and filler entropies both high and nearly equal (~1.3), with near-zero or slightly negative gap, aligning with its failure. This suggests the normalization/ranking choice can destroy the separability of the routing signal.

“Relative Acc vs KV Budget” with α in {0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1.0} shows all curves exactly at 1.0 for all retain ratios and all datasets. This indicates α (presumably mixing routing vs attention signals) has no observable effect under this evaluation regime. Either the tasks are saturated or α is not being applied (e.g., score mixing bug), or the budgets are not aggressive enough to expose differences.

“TopK vs Threshold RouteKV” shows TopK RouteKV and Threshold RouteKV at ~1.0 relative accuracy even at low effective ratios (~0.2 for threshold), while TopK random degrades at low ratios (~0.947–0.958 at 0.25) and recovers near 1.0 by 0.6. This is a meaningful result: routing-guided selection appears to preserve accuracy at much lower budgets than random, suggesting the signal is informative. However, TopK attention also sits at 1.0 everywhere, so the comparison to attention is inconclusive here. Also, the threshold points appear at a single low ratio (~0.2) rather than spanning a range, so the adaptivity story isn’t yet demonstrated.

“Full-Cache Test Accuracy vs Best Validation Accuracy per Dataset” shows both at 1.0 for frontloaded/scattered/multiclass. This indicates perfect accuracy and no generalization gap, reinforcing that the tasks are likely too easy/synthetic and may not be suitable to evaluate nuanced cache-eviction tradeoffs. Consider harder settings (longer contexts, more distractors, partial credit metrics) where full-cache accuracy is <100%.

“Routing Entropy Distribution” bars show mean routing entropy for key tokens is very low (synthetic ~0.072; sst2 ~0.114; ag_news ~0.220) while filler tokens are high (~1.12–1.16). The global mean is ~0.92–0.98. This is strong evidence that the routing entropy signal cleanly separates key vs filler tokens in this setup. The increasing key-token entropy from synthetic→sst2→ag_news may indicate task/domain differences or noisier routing specialization on real text. Still, the separation is large, suggesting RouteKV should work well—though the downstream accuracy plots are saturated and don’t quantify the benefit.

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots largely show saturated accuracy (1.0) across retain ratios and strategies, making most ablations inconclusive. The informative results are: (1) routing entropy strongly separates key vs filler tokens; (2) a ‘rank’ variant catastrophically fails, consistent with selecting the wrong tokens and destroying entropy separability; (3) RouteKV (TopK/threshold) matches full-cache at much lower budgets than random in the TopK-vs-threshold plot. Overall, evaluation appears too easy or eviction may not be applied; need harder benchmarks/longer contexts and sanity checks to reveal tradeoffs and to compare against attention baselines.

overall_plan:
The overarching goal is to develop a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE)-based KV cache eviction mechanism where routing entropy serves as a proxy for token importance, enabling efficient attention by selectively retaining 'key' tokens while evicting 'filler' tokens. The core scientific hypothesis is that tokens routed consistently to specialized experts (low entropy) are more important than tokens spread across experts (high entropy).

The parent plan identified and addressed the fundamental problem of near-uniform routing entropy (~2.02-2.05 nats, near the maximum of ~2.079 nats), which arose because the router had no incentive to specialize. The fixes implemented include: (1) explicit routing specialization loss with KL divergence between key and filler token routing distributions to directly supervise token-type-specific routing behavior; (2) top-1 hard routing to force discrete expert assignments and create larger entropy gaps between important and unimportant tokens; (3) temperature-sharpened routing with token-type supervision; (4) reducing the number of experts to 4 for easier specialization; and (5) an auxiliary routing loss (coefficient λ=0.5) penalizing key tokens for routing to shared experts. The primary validation metric is Spearman correlation between routing specialization scores (negative mean entropy) and attention-based token importance scores. Retention budgets of 25%, 40%, 60%, and 75% are swept to generate full accuracy-compression tradeoff curves, and token-level precision/recall of eviction is computed for synthetic data where ground truth token importance is known.

The current plan extends this work with an ablation study called 'Routing Score Temporal Aggregation Window Ablation,' which investigates which transformer layers' routing information is most informative for KV eviction decisions. Specifically, four layer aggregation strategies are evaluated: (1) 'all' layers, (2) 'early' layers only, (3) 'late' layers only, and (4) the 'last' layer only. A new function `compute_routing_scores_with_strategy` selects different layer subsets before averaging softmax probabilities to compute routing entropy scores. The experimental setup is simplified to use three self-contained synthetic datasets with varying characteristics (rather than external HuggingFace datasets like SST-2 and AG News) to ensure reproducibility. Results are compared across retain ratios using accuracy, Spearman correlation with attention scores, entropy gap between key and filler tokens, and precision/recall for key token retention. This ablation aims to determine the optimal temporal aggregation window for routing-based importance estimation, which has direct implications for the computational efficiency and accuracy of the overall KV cache eviction mechanism.

import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
import numpy as np
import matplotlib

matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
from scipy.stats import spearmanr

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

SEED = 42
torch.manual_seed(SEED)
np.random.seed(SEED)

N_SAMPLES = 3000
SEQ_LEN = 48
D_MODEL = 128
N_HEADS = 4
N_LAYERS = 4
N_EXPERTS = 4
TOP_K = 1
DROPOUT = 0.1
WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4
EPOCHS = 80
BATCH_SIZE = 64
RETAIN_RATIOS = [0.25, 0.40, 0.60, 0.75]
ROUTING_LAMBDA = 0.5
LR = 3e-4
LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES = ["all", "early", "late", "last"]

experiment_data = {}


def make_synthetic_dataset(n, n_classes=4, seq_len=SEQ_LEN, d_model=D_MODEL, seed=0):
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    labels = rng.randint(0, n_classes, size=n)
    basis = np.zeros((n_classes, d_model), dtype=np.float32)
    for c in range(n_classes):
        basis[c, c * (d_model // n_classes) : (c + 1) * (d_model // n_classes)] = 8.0
    key_vecs = basis[labels] + 0.1 * rng.randn(n, d_model).astype(np.float32)
    n_key = max(1, seq_len // 5)
    n_filler = seq_len - n_key
    filler = 0.2 * rng.randn(n, n_filler, d_model).astype(np.float32)
    key_positions = np.zeros((n, n_key, d_model), dtype=np.float32)
    for i in range(n):
        for k in range(n_key):
            key_positions[i, k] = key_vecs[i] + 0.05 * rng.randn(d_model)
    seqs = np.concatenate([key_positions, filler], axis=1).astype(np.float32)
    token_types = np.zeros((n, seq_len), dtype=np.int64)
    token_types[:, :n_key] = 1
    return (
        torch.tensor(seqs),
        torch.tensor(labels.astype(np.int64)),
        torch.tensor(token_types),
        n_key,
    )


class MoELayer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model, n_experts=N_EXPERTS):
        super().__init__()
        self.n_experts = n_experts
        self.gate = nn.Linear(d_model, n_experts, bias=False)
        self.experts = nn.ModuleList(
            [
                nn.Sequential(
                    nn.Linear(d_model, d_model * 2),
                    nn.GELU(),
                    nn.Dropout(DROPOUT),
                    nn.Linear(d_model * 2, d_model),
                )
                for _ in range(n_experts)
            ]
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        B, T, D = x.shape
        logits = self.gate(x)
        idx = logits.argmax(dim=-1)
        out = torch.zeros_like(x)
        flat_x = x.reshape(B * T, D)
        flat_idx = idx.reshape(B * T)
        for e in range(self.n_experts):
            mask = flat_idx == e
            if mask.any():
                out.reshape(B * T, D)[mask] = self.experts[e](flat_x[mask])
        return out, logits


class MoETransformerLayer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model, n_heads):
        super().__init__()
        self.attn = nn.MultiheadAttention(
            d_model, n_heads, dropout=DROPOUT, batch_first=True
        )
        self.moe = MoELayer(d_model)
        self.norm1 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model)
        self.norm2 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(DROPOUT)

    def forward(self, x, return_attn=False):
        attn_out, attn_weights = self.attn(
            x, x, x, need_weights=True, average_attn_weights=True
        )
        x = self.norm1(x + self.drop(attn_out))
        moe_out, router_logits = self.moe(x)
        x = self.norm2(x + moe_out)
        if return_attn:
            return x, router_logits, attn_weights
        return x, router_logits


class MoEClassifier(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self, d_model=D_MODEL, n_heads=N_HEADS, n_layers=N_LAYERS, n_classes=4
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.n_layers = n_layers
        self.proj = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
        self.layers = nn.ModuleList(
            [MoETransformerLayer(d_model, n_heads) for _ in range(n_layers)]
        )
        self.head = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(d_model), nn.Dropout(DROPOUT), nn.Linear(d_model, n_classes)
        )

    def forward(self, x, return_routing=False, return_attn=False):
        x = F.gelu(self.proj(x))
        all_rl, all_aw = [], []
        for layer in self.layers:
            if return_attn:
                x, rl, aw = layer(x, return_attn=True)
                all_aw.append(aw)
            else:
                x, rl = layer(x)
            all_rl.append(rl)
        cls = x.mean(dim=1)
        logits = self.head(cls)
        if return_routing:
            return logits, all_rl, all_aw
        return logits


def routing_specialization_loss(router_logits_list, token_types):
    loss = 0.0
    for rl in router_logits_list:
        key_mask = token_types == 1
        filler_mask = token_types == 0
        if key_mask.any():
            key_logits = rl[key_mask]
            target_key = torch.zeros(
                key_logits.size(0), dtype=torch.long, device=rl.device
            )
            loss += F.cross_entropy(key_logits, target_key)
        if filler_mask.any():
            filler_logits = rl[filler_mask]
            n_e = filler_logits.size(-1)
            target_filler = torch.randint(
                1, n_e, (filler_logits.size(0),), device=rl.device
            )
            loss += F.cross_entropy(filler_logits, target_filler)
    return loss / len(router_logits_list)


def compute_routing_scores_with_strategy(rl_list, strategy="all"):
    n_layers = len(rl_list)
    if strategy == "all":
        selected = rl_list
    elif strategy == "early":
        cutoff = max(1, n_layers // 2)
        selected = rl_list[:cutoff]
    elif strategy == "late":
        cutoff = n_layers // 2
        selected = rl_list[cutoff:]
        if not selected:
            selected = rl_list[-1:]
    elif strategy == "last":
        selected = rl_list[-1:]
    else:
        selected = rl_list
    stacked = torch.stack([F.softmax(rl, dim=-1) for rl in selected], dim=0)
    avg_prob = stacked.mean(0)
    entropy = -(avg_prob * (avg_prob + 1e-9).log()).sum(-1)
    return entropy


def compute_attention_scores(aw_list):
    if not aw_list:
        return None
    attn_stack = torch.stack(aw_list, dim=0)
    return attn_stack.mean(0).sum(2)


def get_keep_mask(scores, retain_ratio, higher_is_better=True):
    B, T = scores.shape
    k = max(1, int(T * retain_ratio))
    if higher_is_better:
        _, topk = torch.topk(scores, k, dim=1)
    else:
        _, topk = torch.topk(-scores, k, dim=1)
    mask = torch.zeros(B, T, dtype=torch.bool, device=scores.device)
    mask.scatter_(1, topk, True)
    return mask


def compute_eviction_precision(model, loader, retain_ratio, n_key, strategy="all"):
    model.eval()
    total_key_retained, total_key, total_retained = 0, 0, 0
    with torch.no_grad():
        for xb, yb, tt in loader:
            xb = xb.to(device)
            tt = tt.to(device)
            _, rl_list, _ = model(xb, return_routing=True, return_attn=True)
            route_ent = compute_routing_scores_with_strategy(rl_list, strategy)
            mask = get_keep_mask(route_ent, retain_ratio, higher_is_better=False)
            key_mask = tt == 1
            retained_keys = (mask & key_mask).sum().item()
            total_key_retained += retained_keys
            total_key += key_mask.sum().item()
            total_retained += mask.sum().item()
    precision = total_key_retained / total_retained if total_retained > 0 else 0
    recall = total_key_retained / total_key if total_key > 0 else 0
    return precision, recall


def evaluate_with_eviction_strategy(model, loader, strategy, retain_ratio):
    model.eval()
    correct, total = 0, 0
    with torch.no_grad():
        for batch in loader:
            xb, yb = batch[0].to(device), batch[1].to(device)
            _, rl_list, aw_list = model(xb, return_routing=True, return_attn=True)
            scores = compute_routing_scores_with_strategy(rl_list, strategy)
            mask = get_keep_mask(scores, retain_ratio, higher_is_better=False)
            x_evicted = xb * mask.unsqueeze(-1).float()
            logits = model(x_evicted)
            correct += (logits.argmax(1) == yb).sum().item()
            total += xb.size(0)
    return correct / total


def compute_spearman_corr_strategy(model, loader, strategy="all"):
    model.eval()
    all_route_scores, all_attn_scores = [], []
    with torch.no_grad():
        for xb, yb, tt in loader:
            xb = xb.to(device)
            _, rl_list, aw_list = model(xb, return_routing=True, return_attn=True)
            route_ent = compute_routing_scores_with_strategy(rl_list, strategy)
            attn_imp = compute_attention_scores(aw_list)
            all_route_scores.append(route_ent.cpu().numpy().flatten())
            all_attn_scores.append(attn_imp.cpu().numpy().flatten())
    route_flat = np.concatenate(all_route_scores)
    attn_flat = np.concatenate(all_attn_scores)
    spec_flat = -route_flat
    corr, pval = spearmanr(spec_flat, attn_flat)
    return corr, pval


def run_ablation_experiment(
    dataset_name, train_loader, val_loader, test_loader, n_classes, n_key
):
    print(f"\n--- Dataset: {dataset_name} ---")
    torch.manual_seed(SEED)
    model = MoEClassifier(n_classes=n_classes).to(device)
    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=LR, weight_decay=WEIGHT_DECAY)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=EPOCHS)
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()

    train_losses, val_losses, train_accs, val_accs = [], [], [], []
    best_val_acc, best_state = 0.0, None

    for epoch in range(1, EPOCHS + 1):
        model.train()
        tr_loss, tr_correct, tr_total = 0.0, 0, 0
        for batch in train_loader:
            xb, yb, tt = batch[0].to(device), batch[1].to(device), batch[2].to(device)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            logits, rl_list, _ = model(xb, return_routing=True, return_attn=False)
            ce_loss = criterion(logits, yb)
            rs_loss = routing_specialization_loss(rl_list, tt)
            loss = ce_loss + ROUTING_LAMBDA * rs_loss
            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            optimizer.step()
            tr_loss += ce_loss.item() * xb.size(0)
            tr_correct += (logits.argmax(1) == yb).sum().item()
            tr_total += xb.size(0)
        scheduler.step()

        model.eval()
        val_loss, val_correct, val_total = 0.0, 0, 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for batch in val_loader:
                xb, yb = batch[0].to(device), batch[1].to(device)
                logits = model(xb)
                val_loss += criterion(logits, yb).item() * xb.size(0)
                val_correct += (logits.argmax(1) == yb).sum().item()
                val_total += xb.size(0)

        tr_acc = tr_correct / tr_total
        val_acc = val_correct / val_total
        train_losses.append(tr_loss / tr_total)
        val_losses.append(val_loss / val_total)
        train_accs.append(tr_acc)
        val_accs.append(val_acc)

        if epoch % 20 == 0 or epoch == EPOCHS:
            print(
                f"Epoch {epoch:03d}: val_loss={val_loss/val_total:.4f} val_acc={val_acc:.4f}"
            )

        if val_acc > best_val_acc:
            best_val_acc = val_acc
            best_state = {k: v.cpu().clone() for k, v in model.state_dict().items()}

    model.load_state_dict({k: v.to(device) for k, v in best_state.items()})

    # Full-cache accuracy
    model.eval()
    full_correct, full_total = 0, 0
    with torch.no_grad():
        for batch in test_loader:
            xb, yb = batch[0].to(device), batch[1].to(device)
            logits = model(xb)
            full_correct += (logits.argmax(1) == yb).sum().item()
            full_total += xb.size(0)
    full_acc = full_correct / full_total
    print(f"Full-cache test acc: {full_acc:.4f}")

    # Evaluate each layer aggregation strategy
    strategy_results = {}
    for strategy in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES:
        eviction_accs = {}
        for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS:
            acc = evaluate_with_eviction_strategy(model, test_loader, strategy, rr)
            eviction_accs[rr] = acc

        sp_corr, sp_pval = compute_spearman_corr_strategy(model, test_loader, strategy)

        precision_recall = {}
        for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS:
            p, r = compute_eviction_precision(model, test_loader, rr, n_key, strategy)
            precision_recall[rr] = {"precision": p, "recall": r}

        model.eval()
        key_ents, filler_ents = [], []
        with torch.no_grad():
            for batch in test_loader:
                xb, yb, tt = (
                    batch[0].to(device),
                    batch[1].to(device),
                    batch[2].to(device),
                )
                _, rl_list, _ = model(xb, return_routing=True, return_attn=False)
                ent = compute_routing_scores_with_strategy(rl_list, strategy)
                key_mask = tt == 1
                filler_mask = tt == 0
                key_ents.append(ent[key_mask].cpu())
                filler_ents.append(ent[filler_mask].cpu())
        key_ent = torch.cat(key_ents).mean().item()
        filler_ent = torch.cat(filler_ents).mean().item()
        ent_gap = filler_ent - key_ent

        strategy_results[strategy] = {
            "eviction_accs": eviction_accs,
            "spearman": sp_corr,
            "spearman_pval": sp_pval,
            "routing_entropy": {"key": key_ent, "filler": filler_ent, "gap": ent_gap},
            "precision_recall": {str(k): v for k, v in precision_recall.items()},
        }
        print(
            f"  Strategy={strategy}: spearman={sp_corr:.4f} entropy_gap={ent_gap:.4f}"
        )
        for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS:
            acc = eviction_accs[rr]
            rel = acc / full_acc if full_acc > 0 else 0
            p = precision_recall[rr]["precision"]
            r = precision_recall[rr]["recall"]
            print(
                f"    retain={rr:.2f}: acc={acc:.4f} rel={rel:.4f} prec={p:.4f} recall={r:.4f}"
            )

    return {
        "full_acc": full_acc,
        "best_val_acc": best_val_acc,
        "metrics": {"train": train_accs, "val": val_accs},
        "losses": {"train": train_losses, "val": val_losses},
        "strategy_results": strategy_results,
    }


# Prepare datasets
print("Preparing datasets...")
X_syn, y_syn, tt_syn, nk_syn = make_synthetic_dataset(N_SAMPLES, seed=0)
X_sv, y_sv, tt_sv, _ = make_synthetic_dataset(600, seed=1)
X_st, y_st, tt_st, _ = make_synthetic_dataset(600, seed=2)
n_classes_syn = 4
syn_train = DataLoader(
    TensorDataset(X_syn, y_syn, tt_syn), batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True
)
syn_val = DataLoader(TensorDataset(X_sv, y_sv, tt_sv), batch_size=BATCH_SIZE)
syn_test = DataLoader(TensorDataset(X_st, y_st, tt_st), batch_size=BATCH_SIZE)

print("Creating Synthetic Dataset 2 (higher noise)...")
rng2 = np.random.RandomState(10)
n2 = N_SAMPLES
labels2 = rng2.randint(0, 4, size=n2)
basis2 = np.zeros((4, D_MODEL), dtype=np.float32)
for c in range(4):
    basis2[c, c * (D_MODEL // 4) : (c + 1) * (D_MODEL // 4)] = 5.0
key_vecs2 = basis2[labels2] + 0.5 * rng2.randn(n2, D_MODEL).astype(np.float32)
n_key2 = max(1, SEQ_LEN // 5)
n_filler2 = SEQ_LEN - n_key2
filler2 = 0.8 * rng2.randn(n2, n_filler2, D_MODEL).astype(np.float32)
key_pos2 = np.zeros((n2, n_key2, D_MODEL), dtype=np.float32)
for i in range(n2):
    for k in range(n_key2):
        key_pos2[i, k] = key_vecs2[i] + 0.2 * rng2.randn(D_MODEL)
seqs2 = np.concatenate([key_pos2, filler2], axis=1).astype(np.float32)
tt2 = np.zeros((n2, SEQ_LEN), dtype=np.int64)
tt2[:, :n_key2] = 1
X_syn2 = torch.tensor(seqs2)
y_syn2 = torch.tensor(labels2.astype(np.int64))
tt_syn2 = torch.tensor(tt2)
syn2_val_idx, syn2_test_idx = 600, 600
syn2_train = DataLoader(
    TensorDataset(
        X_syn2[: n2 - syn2_val_idx - syn2_test_idx],
        y_syn2[: n2 - syn2_val_idx - syn2_test_idx],
        tt_syn2[: n2 - syn2_val_idx - syn2_test_idx],
    ),
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    shuffle=True,
)
syn2_val = DataLoader(
    TensorDataset(
        X_syn2[n2 - syn2_val_idx - syn2_test_idx : n2 - syn2_test_idx],
        y_syn2[n2 - syn2_val_idx - syn2_test_idx : n2 - syn2_test_idx],
        tt_syn2[n2 - syn2_val_idx - syn2_test_idx : n2 - syn2_test_idx],
    ),
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
)
syn2_test = DataLoader(
    TensorDataset(
        X_syn2[n2 - syn2_test_idx :],
        y_syn2[n2 - syn2_test_idx :],
        tt_syn2[n2 - syn2_test_idx :],
    ),
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
)

print("Creating Synthetic Dataset 3 (longer key region)...")
rng3 = np.random.RandomState(20)
n3 = N_SAMPLES
labels3 = rng3.randint(0, 4, size=n3)
basis3 = np.zeros((4, D_MODEL), dtype=np.float32)
for c in range(4):
    basis3[c, c * (D_MODEL // 4) : (c + 1) * (D_MODEL // 4)] = 8.0
key_vecs3 = basis3[labels3] + 0.1 * rng3.randn(n3, D_MODEL).astype(np.float32)
n_key3 = max(1, SEQ_LEN * 2 // 5)
n_filler3 = SEQ_LEN - n_key3
filler3 = 0.2 * rng3.randn(n3, n_filler3, D_MODEL).astype(np.float32)
key_pos3 = np.zeros((n3, n_key3, D_MODEL), dtype=np.float32)
for i in range(n3):
    for k in range(n_key3):
        key_pos3[i, k] = key_vecs3[i] + 0.05 * rng3.randn(D_MODEL)
seqs3 = np.concatenate([key_pos3, filler3], axis=1).astype(np.float32)
tt3 = np.zeros((n3, SEQ_LEN), dtype=np.int64)
tt3[:, :n_key3] = 1
X_syn3 = torch.tensor(seqs3)
y_syn3 = torch.tensor(labels3.astype(np.int64))
tt_syn3 = torch.tensor(tt3)
syn3_val_idx, syn3_test_idx = 600, 600
syn3_train = DataLoader(
    TensorDataset(
        X_syn3[: n3 - syn3_val_idx - syn3_test_idx],
        y_syn3[: n3 - syn3_val_idx - syn3_test_idx],
        tt_syn3[: n3 - syn3_val_idx - syn3_test_idx],
    ),
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    shuffle=True,
)
syn3_val = DataLoader(
    TensorDataset(
        X_syn3[n3 - syn3_val_idx - syn3_test_idx : n3 - syn3_test_idx],
        y_syn3[n3 - syn3_val_idx - syn3_test_idx : n3 - syn3_test_idx],
        tt_syn3[n3 - syn3_val_idx - syn3_test_idx : n3 - syn3_test_idx],
    ),
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
)
syn3_test = DataLoader(

        X_syn3[n3 - syn3_test_idx :],
        y_syn3[n3 - syn3_test_idx :],
        tt_syn3[n3 - syn3_test_idx :],
    ),
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
)

DATASETS = {
    "synthetic_standard": (syn_train, syn_val, syn_test, n_classes_syn, nk_syn),
    "synthetic_high_noise": (syn2_train, syn2_val, syn2_test, 4, n_key2),
    "synthetic_long_key": (syn3_train, syn3_val, syn3_test, 4, n_key3),
}

all_results = {}
for ds_name, (tr_l, va_l, te_l, nc, nk) in DATASETS.items():
    res = run_ablation_experiment(ds_name, tr_l, va_l, te_l, nc, nk)

    all_results[ds_name] = res

# ======== Visualization ========
colors_strategy = {

    "early": "darkorange",
    "late": "seagreen",
    "last": "crimson",
}
ds_names = list(DATASETS.keys())

# Plot 1: Accuracy vs retain ratio per dataset per strategy
for ds_name in ds_names:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
    d = all_results[ds_name]
    full_acc = d["full_acc"]
    ax.axhline(full_acc, color="black", linestyle="--", label="Full Cache", linewidth=2)
    for strategy in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES:
        accs = [

        ]
        ax.plot(
            RETAIN_RATIOS,
            accs,
            marker="o",
            label=f"{strategy}",
            color=colors_strategy[strategy],
            linewidth=2,
        )
    ax.set_xlabel("Retain Ratio (KV Budget)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Accuracy")
    ax.set_title(f"Layer Aggregation Strategy - Accuracy vs KV Budget\n({ds_name})")
    ax.legend()
    ax.set_xlim([0.2, 0.8])
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, f"compression_curve_{ds_name}.png"), dpi=100)
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: Training curves per dataset

for ds_name in ds_names:

    axes[0].plot(epochs_range, d["losses"]["train"], label="Train", color="steelblue")
    axes[0].plot(epochs_range, d["losses"]["val"], label="Val", color="darkorange")

    axes[1].set_ylabel("Accuracy")
    axes[1].legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, f"curves_{ds_name}.png"), dpi=100)
    plt.close()
    print(f"Saved curves_{ds_name}.png")

# Plot 3: Entropy gap per strategy per dataset
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ds_names), figsize=(6 * len(ds_names), 5))
if len(ds_names) == 1:

for col, ds_name in enumerate(ds_names):

    d = all_results[ds_name]

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "entropy_gap_by_strategy.png"), dpi=100)
plt.close()
print("Saved entropy_gap_by_strategy.png")

# Plot 4: Spearman correlation per strategy per dataset
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ds_names), figsize=(6 * len(ds_names), 5))
if len(ds_names) == 1:
    axes = [axes]
for col, ds_name in enumerate(ds_names):
    ax = axes[col]
    d = all_results[ds_name]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ds_names), figsize=(6 * len(ds_names), 5))
if len(ds_names) == 1:

n_strat = len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)
width = 0.18
for i, strategy in enumerate(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES):
    rel_accs = []
    for ds_name in ds_names:
        d = all_results[ds_name]
        full_acc = d["full_acc"]
        acc_40 = d["strategy_results"][strategy]["eviction_accs"][0.40]
        rel_accs.append(acc_40 / full_acc if full_acc > 0 else 0)
    offset = (i - n_strat / 2 + 0.5) * width
    bars = ax.bar(
        x_ds + offset,
        rel_accs,
        width,
        label=strategy,
        color=colors_strategy[strategy],
        alpha=0.85,
    )
    for bar, val in zip(bars, rel_accs):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.005,
            f"{val:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=7,
        )
ax.axhline(1.0, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.5, label="Full Cache")
ax.set_xticks(x_ds)
ax.set_xticklabels(ds_names, rotation=10)
ax.set_ylabel("Relative Accuracy (vs Full Cache)")
ax.set_title("Layer Aggregation Strategy Comparison @ 40% KV Budget")
ax.legend(title="Layer Strategy", fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylim([0, 1.15])
plt.tight_layout()

plt.close()

# Plot 7: Heatmap of relative accuracy across strategies and retain ratios (per dataset)
for ds_name in ds_names:
    d = all_results[ds_name]
    full_acc = d["full_acc"]
    matrix = np.zeros((len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES), len(RETAIN_RATIOS)))
    for i, strategy in enumerate(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES):
        for j, rr in enumerate(RETAIN_RATIOS):
            acc = d["strategy_results"][strategy]["eviction_accs"][rr]
            matrix[i, j] = acc / full_acc if full_acc > 0 else 0

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
    im = ax.imshow(matrix, aspect="auto", cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=0.7, vmax=1.05)
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(RETAIN_RATIOS)))
    ax.set_xticklabels([f"{rr:.2f}" for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS])
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)
    ax.set_xlabel("Retain Ratio")
    ax.set_ylabel("Layer Strategy")
    ax.set_title(f"Relative Accuracy Heatmap\n({ds_name})")
    plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax)
    for i in range(len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)):
        for j in range(len(RETAIN_RATIOS)):
            ax.text(
                j,
                i,
                f"{matrix[i, j]:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                fontsize=9,
                color="black",
            )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, f"heatmap_{ds_name}.png"), dpi=100)
    plt.close()
    print(f"Saved heatmap_{ds_name}.png")

# Save results
np.save(os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), experiment_data)
print("\nAll experiment data saved.")

# Print final summary
print("\n=== FINAL ABLATION RESULTS: Layer Aggregation Strategy ===")
for ds_name in ds_names:
    r = all_results[ds_name]
    print(f"\n[{ds_name}]  full_acc={r['full_acc']:.4f}")
    for strategy in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES:
        sr = r["strategy_results"][strategy]
        print(f"  Strategy={strategy}:")
        print(f"    Spearman r={sr['spearman']:.4f} (p={sr['spearman_pval']:.4f})")
        print(
            f"    Entropy gap={sr['routing_entropy']['gap']:.4f}  "
            f"(key={sr['routing_entropy']['key']:.4f}, filler={sr['routing_entropy']['filler']:.4f})"
        )
        for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS:
            acc = sr["eviction_accs"][rr]
            rel = acc / r["full_acc"] if r["full_acc"] > 0 else 0
            prec = sr["precision_recall"][str(rr)]["precision"]
            rec = sr["precision_recall"][str(rr)]["recall"]
            print(
                f"    retain={rr:.2f}: acc={acc:.4f} rel={rel:.4f} prec={prec:.4f} rec={rec:.4f}"
            )

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()

except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES = ["all", "early", "late", "last"]
RETAIN_RATIOS = [0.25, 0.40, 0.60, 0.75]
colors_strategy = {
    "all": "steelblue",
    "early": "darkorange",
    "late": "seagreen",
    "last": "crimson",
}
ds_names = list(experiment_data.keys())
EPOCHS = 80
epochs_range = list(range(1, EPOCHS + 1))

# Plot 1: Training/Validation Loss and Accuracy curves per dataset
for ds_name in ds_names:
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
        d = experiment_data[ds_name]
        axes[0].plot(
            epochs_range, d["losses"]["train"], label="Train", color="steelblue"
        )
        axes[0].plot(
            epochs_range, d["losses"]["val"], label="Validation", color="darkorange"
        )
        axes[0].set_title(f"Loss Curves\nDataset: {ds_name}")
        axes[0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
        axes[0].set_ylabel("Cross-Entropy Loss")
        axes[0].legend()
        axes[1].plot(
            epochs_range, d["metrics"]["train"], label="Train", color="steelblue"
        )
        axes[1].plot(
            epochs_range, d["metrics"]["val"], label="Validation", color="darkorange"
        )
        axes[1].set_title(f"Accuracy Curves\nDataset: {ds_name}")
        axes[1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
        axes[1].set_ylabel("Accuracy")
        axes[1].legend()
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"training_curves_{ds_name}.png"), dpi=100
        )
        plt.close()
        print(f"Saved training_curves_{ds_name}.png")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating training curves for {ds_name}: {e}")
        plt.close()

# Plot 2: Accuracy vs Retain Ratio (Compression Curves) per dataset
for ds_name in ds_names:
    try:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
        d = experiment_data[ds_name]
        full_acc = d["full_acc"]
        ax.axhline(
            full_acc, color="black", linestyle="--", label="Full Cache", linewidth=2
        )
        for strategy in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES:
            accs = [

                for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS

            ax.plot(

        ax.set_title(
            f"Layer Aggregation Strategy: Accuracy vs KV Retain Ratio\nDataset: {ds_name}"
        )
        ax.legend(title="Strategy")
        ax.set_xlim([0.2, 0.8])
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(

        plt.close()
        print(f"Saved compression_curve_{ds_name}.png")
    except Exception as e:

# Plot 5: Precision and Recall @ 40% Retain Ratio per strategy per dataset

except Exception as e:

# Plot 6: Summary - Relative accuracy @ 40% retain across datasets and strategies
try:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

        full_acc = d["full_acc"]
        matrix = np.zeros((len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES), len(RETAIN_RATIOS)))
        for i, strategy in enumerate(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES):
            for j, rr in enumerate(RETAIN_RATIOS):
                acc = d["strategy_results"][strategy]["eviction_accs"][rr]
                matrix[i, j] = acc / full_acc if full_acc > 0 else 0
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
        im = ax.imshow(matrix, aspect="auto", cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=0.7, vmax=1.05)
        ax.set_xticks(range(len(RETAIN_RATIOS)))
        ax.set_xticklabels([f"{rr:.2f}" for rr in RETAIN_RATIOS])
        ax.set_yticks(range(len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)))
        ax.set_yticklabels(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)
        ax.set_xlabel("Retain Ratio (KV Budget)")
        ax.set_ylabel("Layer Aggregation Strategy")
        ax.set_title(
            f"Relative Accuracy Heatmap (Strategy × Retain Ratio)\nDataset: {ds_name}"
        )
        plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="Relative Accuracy vs Full Cache")
        for i in range(len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)):
            for j in range(len(RETAIN_RATIOS)):
                ax.text(
                    j,
                    i,
                    f"{matrix[i, j]:.3f}",
                    ha="center",
                    va="center",
                    fontsize=9,
                    color="black",
                )
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"heatmap_relative_accuracy_{ds_name}.png"),
            dpi=100,
        )
        plt.close()
        print(f"Saved heatmap_relative_accuracy_{ds_name}.png")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating heatmap for {ds_name}: {e}")
        plt.close()

# Plot 8: Key vs Filler Routing Entropy per strategy per dataset
for ds_name in ds_names:
    try:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
        d = experiment_data[ds_name]
        x_pos = np.arange(len(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES))
        key_ents = [
            d["strategy_results"][s]["routing_entropy"]["key"]
            for s in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES
        ]
        filler_ents = [
            d["strategy_results"][s]["routing_entropy"]["filler"]
            for s in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES
        ]
        width = 0.35
        ax.bar(
            x_pos - width / 2,
            key_ents,
            width,
            label="Key Tokens",
            color=[colors_strategy[s] for s in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES],
            alpha=0.9,
        )
        ax.bar(
            x_pos + width / 2,
            filler_ents,
            width,
            label="Filler Tokens",
            color=[colors_strategy[s] for s in LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES],
            alpha=0.4,
            edgecolor="black",
        )
        ax.set_xticks(x_pos)
        ax.set_xticklabels(LAYER_AGG_STRATEGIES)
        ax.set_ylabel("Mean Routing Entropy")
        ax.set_title(
            f"Key vs Filler Token Routing Entropy by Layer Strategy\nDataset: {ds_name}"
        )
        ax.legend()
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, f"routing_entropy_key_vs_filler_{ds_name}.png"),
            dpi=100,
        )
        plt.close()
        print(f"Saved routing_entropy_key_vs_filler_{ds_name}.png")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating routing entropy plot for {ds_name}: {e}")
        plt.close()
plot_plan: The experiment trains a MoE (Mixture of Experts) Transformer classifier on three synthetic datasets and evaluates different layer aggregation strategies ("all", "early", "late", "last") for KV cache token eviction. I need to plot: training/validation loss and accuracy curves per dataset, compression curves (accuracy vs retain ratio) per strategy per dataset, entropy gap bar charts, Spearman correlation bars, precision/recall at 40% retain, a summary comparison bar chart, and heatmaps of relative accuracy. I'll load the experiment data and create each plot in a separate try-except block, saving all figures to the working directory.

plot_analyses.analysis: At 40% KV budget, relative accuracy is exactly 1.000 for every layer aggregation strategy (all/early/late/last) across all three synthetic datasets (standard, high_noise, long_key). This indicates the task is too easy or the eviction is not actually stressing the model at this budget—no strategy-level differences can be inferred from accuracy here. For an ablation intended to show component contribution, this plot currently provides no discriminative evidence; you’ll need either lower KV budgets, harder datasets, or a metric beyond end-task accuracy (e.g., logprob degradation, calibration, or needle position sensitivity).

Accuracy vs retain ratio on synthetic_long_key is perfectly flat at 1.0 from 0.25 to 0.75 retain, and the curves for all strategies overlap. This suggests either (a) the task remains solvable with very aggressive eviction, (b) the evaluation is insensitive (e.g., small test set, deterministic shortcut), or (c) the eviction policy is not actually removing information needed for this dataset. If the hypothesis is about routing capturing importance, you need regimes where retaining the wrong tokens hurts (e.g., multiple keys, adversarial distractors, longer dependency distance, or evaluation that requires exact retrieval rather than coarse accuracy).

Routing entropy gap (filler − key) varies meaningfully by layer aggregation strategy and dataset, even though accuracy does not. On synthetic_standard: early has the smallest gap (~1.009), while late/last are larger (~1.085–1.089), suggesting later-layer routing patterns separate key vs filler tokens more strongly. On synthetic_high_noise: early is markedly lower (~0.823) than late/last (~1.028–1.038), indicating early-layer routing becomes less informative under noise, while late/last remain robust. On synthetic_long_key: early again lower (~0.878) vs late/last (~1.045–1.049). Takeaway: aggregating later layers (or emphasizing last layers) yields a cleaner importance signal by this proxy, especially in noisy settings—supporting the design choice to aggregate across layers but perhaps with a late-layer weighting rather than uniform averaging.

Spearman correlation between routing specialization and attention is extremely close to zero across datasets and strategies (values roughly between −0.001 and 0.007). This is an important negative result: routing-derived importance appears largely orthogonal to attention-based importance, supporting the paper’s claim of complementarity (hybrid scoring could add value). However, the near-zero correlations also raise a question: if routing and attention disagree, which aligns better with ground-truth token importance? You partially address this with key-token precision/recall, but you may also want AUC/average precision over token-importance labels and show which signal is more predictive under stress (lower budgets).

Key-token precision/recall at 40% retain: on synthetic_standard and synthetic_high_noise, recall is 1.0 for all strategies while precision is ~0.47–0.48. This implies the policy always keeps all key tokens but also keeps many filler tokens (expected when retaining 40% and keys are a small fraction). On synthetic_long_key, both precision and recall are 1.0 for all strategies, indicating perfect separation between key and filler tokens for this dataset at this budget. Since accuracy is saturated, these token-level metrics are currently the main evidence; still, because recall is trivially perfect across strategies, the ablation doesn’t differentiate components. Consider reporting precision@k across a sweep of budgets (e.g., 5–40%) or using F1/AUPRC to reveal differences.

Relative accuracy heatmap for synthetic_long_key is uniformly 1.000 for all strategies and retain ratios shown (0.25, 0.40, 0.60, 0.75). This reinforces that the evaluation is not in a challenging regime. For a convincing ablation, you’d want to see a phase transition where accuracy drops as budget shrinks, and strategies shift that curve. If the method is intended for long-context stress, try much lower retains (e.g., 1–10%), longer contexts, or tasks with multiple needles and confounders.

Mean routing entropy for key vs filler tokens on synthetic_long_key shows very strong separability for every layer strategy: key tokens have very low entropy (~0.07–0.27 depending on strategy) while filler tokens are high (~1.11–1.15). Late/last strategies produce the lowest key-token entropy (~0.06–0.08), while early yields higher key entropy (~0.27), matching the earlier ‘entropy gap’ plot. This supports the core hypothesis that key tokens are more specialized (lower entropy) and that later-layer routing captures this more sharply. It also suggests a practical tweak: use a late-layer-weighted entropy or minimum-over-layers entropy to sharpen key-token identification.

Compression curves by gate placement (post_attention, pre_attention, dual_gate) on the synthetic dataset are identical and flat at 1.0 accuracy across retain ratios (0.25–0.75). As with prior accuracy plots, this provides no evidence to choose a gate placement based on end performance in this setting. The implication is that gate placement decisions must be justified via other metrics (signal quality, stability, compute/engineering constraints) or evaluated on harder tasks where eviction matters.

Gate placement ablation summary: (i) Spearman corr (routing spec vs attention) varies by dataset and placement: on sst2, post_attention is notably higher (~0.023) than pre_attention (~0.0055) and dual (~0.007), while on synthetic pre_attention is slightly negative (~−0.01). (ii) Routing entropy gap (filler−key) is consistently largest for pre_attention across datasets (synthetic ~1.22, sst2 ~1.20, ag_news ~1.15), suggesting pre-attention routing yields the strongest separability between key and filler tokens by this metric; post_attention and dual are lower. (iii) RouteKV relative accuracy @40% is 1.0 for all placements/datasets; full-cache accuracy is also 1.0. (iv) Key-token precision @40% is identical across placements (~0.47), so separability gains aren’t translating into higher precision at this budget. (v) Key-token routing entropy differs: pre_attention yields the lowest key entropy across datasets (e.g., synthetic ~0.04 vs post ~0.07 vs dual ~0.095; ag_news ~0.15 vs ~0.22 vs ~0.285). Overall: pre_attention seems best if your goal is a crisp routing-based importance signal; post_attention aligns more with attention on sst2. Given complementarity claims, you might pick pre_attention for eviction quality and still combine with attention for shared-attention layers.

Training curves (synthetic) by gate placement: validation loss rapidly decays toward ~0 for all placements; dual_gate shows larger early spikes but converges similarly. Validation accuracy is a flat 1.0 throughout training for all placements, again indicating the dataset/model setup is not challenging enough to expose differences. If these are learned routers/gates, you may want to (a) increase task difficulty, (b) reduce model capacity, (c) add longer contexts/noise, or (d) evaluate with more sensitive metrics (e.g., perplexity on held-out, or targeted retrieval accuracy at varying needle depths) to see whether gate placement affects generalization or robustness.

vlm_feedback_summary: Received 10 plots covering (i) layer-aggregation strategy ablations for routing-entropy-based token importance, (ii) accuracy vs KV retain ratio and heatmaps, (iii) routing-entropy gap (filler-key), Spearman correlation between routing specialization and attention, (iv) key-token precision/recall at 40% retain, and (v) gate-placement ablations (pre/post/dual) including compression curves, entropy gaps, correlations, and training curves. Overall, accuracy is saturated at 1.0 across budgets/strategies on these synthetic tasks, while routing-entropy metrics show separability between key vs filler tokens and differences across aggregation/gate placement; routing-vs-attention correlations are near zero.
ablation_name: Routing Score Temporal Aggregation Window Ablation
